title: Conformal removability of non-simple Schramm-Loewner evolutions
speaker: Konstantinos Kavvadias
abstract: We consider the Schramm-Loewner evolution (SLE_{kappa}) for kappa in (4,8), which is the regime that the curve is self-intersecting but not space-filling. We let K be the set of kappa in (4,8) for which the adjacency graph of connected components of the complement of an SLE_{kappa} is almost surely connected, in the sense that for every pair of complementary components U,V there exist complementary components U_1,...,U_n with U_1 = U, U_n = V, and the boundaries of U_i and U_{i+1} intersect for each i between 1 and n-1.

proof_stages.label: Problem motivation from welding and known removability criteria
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls conformal removability and conformal welding, reviews the Jones-Smirnov Hölder-domain criterion and known SLE cases, and isolates the obstruction in the non-simple range ba in (4,8): the SLE trace is no longer the boundary of a simply connected domain.

proof_stages.label: Adjacency hypothesis and main theorem
proof_stages.summary: A connectedness condition is formulated for the adjacency graph of complementary components, the admissible set of ba values is defined, and the main theorem states that chordal SLE_ba is almost surely conformally removable for those ba in (4,8).

proof_stages.label: Deterministic annulus criterion implies ACL and removability
proof_stages.summary: A deterministic removability criterion is introduced using subquadratic Minkowski dimension, annuli around every point, and a path-image diameter bound controlled by the L2 norm of f', and the criterion is shown to force the ACL property by summing variation over small intervals meeting the exceptional set.

proof_stages.label: Abstract path construction from chains of domains
proof_stages.summary: To obtain the paths required in the annulus criterion, the speaker introduces chains of simply connected subsets with intersecting boundaries, measures on the intersection segments, and hyperbolic-geodesic estimates whose concatenation gives an annulus-crossing path satisfying the needed bound.

proof_stages.label: Local GFF flow-line connectivity package
proof_stages.summary: The proof is reduced to verifying a local annular connectivity statement for graphs built from GFF flow lines and counterflow lines, together with Minkowski-dimension and Hölder-continuity controls that imply the hyperbolic-geodesic conditions in the deterministic criterion.

proof_stages.label: Reduction of local connectivity to CLE and the adjacency assumption
proof_stages.summary: By changing GFF boundary conditions, representing counterflow lines as SLE_{ba'}(ba'-6), inducting down to a single branch, and coupling that branch to a CLE exploration tree, local connectivity is proved using the assumed global adjacency connectedness for ordinary SLE_{ba'} excursions.

proof_stages.label: Transfer from flow-line graphs to the actual SLE trace across scales
proof_stages.summary: The SLE trace is coupled as a GFF counterflow line, absolute-continuity estimates compare the field on a dense family of annuli with zero-boundary GFFs, and flow-line interaction rules ensure the constructed chains give simply connected subsets in the complement of the SLE trace.

proof_stages.label: Cut-point measures for boundary intersections
proof_stages.summary: The required measures on intersections of neighboring component boundaries are reduced to cut-point measures for chordal SLE_{ba'}, constructed via an LQG/GFF approximation by counting quantum-area bubbles, conditioned to depend only on the curve, and shown to satisfy the needed lower and upper mass bounds.

examples.name: Known removable simple/critical SLE cases
examples.note: The already-known conformal removability of SLE for ba<4 via Hölder domains, and later ba=4, is used as a contrast showing why the non-simple range needs a new criterion.
examples.engagement_reason: the simple and critical SLE removability results are cited as known motivating cases, with only stated facts about Hölder domains and SLE4 removability

examples.name: Quantum-gravity welding and stable loop trees
examples.note: The LQG welding constructions for simple SLEs and the stable-loop-tree welding for ba in (4,8) motivate why uniqueness from conformal removability is important, but they are not used in the proof described here.
examples.engagement_reason: quantum-gravity welding and stable loop trees are described as known constructions producing SLE interfaces, without carrying out the welding or computations on them

examples.name: Sierpiński carpet
examples.note: The standard Sierpiński carpet illustrates that sets outside the Jones-Smirnov simply-connected-boundary framework can be non-removable, by a Cantor-product/Cantor-function construction.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker sketches why the Sierpiński carpet is nonremovable by using its Cantor-set-product subset and the Cantor function to build a nonconformal homeomorphism

passes.reason: The standard Sierpiński carpet is a regular self-similar fractal used as a non-removable set outside the Jones–Smirnov setting.

examples.name: Sierpiński gasket
examples.note: The gasket is compared with non-simple SLE because complementary-component boundaries intersect, but only in single points rather than in uncountable-dimensional sets, explaining why the new SLE criterion should not accidentally prove removability of the gasket.
examples.engagement_reason: the Sierpiński gasket is used mainly as a comparison/analogy, with stated facts about intersecting complementary-component boundaries and known nonremovability
